title: sendRawTransaction with skipPreflight: true — a failing transaction is broadcast with no simulation, so errors surface silently (or never)

params.passDetail: sendRawTransaction runs preflight simulation (skipPreflight is not true) — a transaction that would fail is caught before it is broadcast and its error is surfaced to the caller.
params.failDetail: sendRawTransaction is called with skipPreflight: true, so the client broadcasts the transaction WITHOUT the preflight simulation that normally catches a failing instruction. A transaction that reverts on-chain is sent anyway; the caller gets a signature and assumes success, while the intended state change (a transfer, a swap, a mint) silently never happened. Remove skipPreflight: true unless you independently simulate and confirm the transaction.
params.absentCallDetail: No sendRawTransaction call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: sendRawTransaction does not set skipPreflight; the default (run preflight) applies.

provenance.note: skipPreflight: true is pasted from throughput-tuning guides to shave latency, but it removes the simulation that catches a doomed transaction. Combined with not confirming the signature, a reverted transfer/swap looks successful — the silent-failure footgun the unconfirmed-state class is about.
